case_title: ROBERTO BARAJAS VS. SHI YUAN KUANG

MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-11-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-11-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER RE MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT/JUDGMENT AGAINST SHI YUAN KUANG

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SHI YUAN KUANG AND ALLISON WONG'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS AND PRAYER IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ROBERTO BARAJAS IS DENIED. WHILE PERHAPS NOT ARTFULLY ALLEGED, LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, THE ALLEGATION THAT MR. BARAJAS WAS TOLD THAT MS. KUANG CONSUMED ALCOHOL PRIOR TO THE COLLISION ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT MS. KUANG ACTED DESPICABLY AND IN WILLFUL DISREGARD OF THE SAFETY OF THOSE SHE MIGHT ENCOUNTER WHEN SHE WAS KNOWINGLY DRIVING A VEHICLE IN AN IMPAIRED CONDITION. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/HEK)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND (TRANSACTION ID # 62386255) FILED BY DEFENDANT KUANG, SHI YUAN HEARING SET FOR SEP-11-2018 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
DECLARATION OF SHI YUAN KUANG AND CHRISTINA E. KIM IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO SET ASIDE REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 62386255) FILED BY DEFENDANT KUANG, SHI YUAN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SHI YUAN KUANG'S MOTION TO QUASH SUMMONS IS DENIED. THE DEFAULT AS TO WONG IS HEREBY VACATED PURSUANT TO CODE CIV. PROC. 473(D). (ELLARD V. CONWAY (2001) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 540, 544, [" A DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST A DEFENDANT WHO WAS NOT SERVED WITH A SUMMONS IN THE MANNER PRESCRIBED BY STATUTE IS VOID."]). COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE ORDER IS TO BE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ANNE-CRISTINE MASSULLO, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. =(302/ACM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALLISON WONG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST ALLISON WONG IS GRANTED. IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON DEFENDANT ALLISON WONG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS, THE DEFAULT IS VACATED PURSUANT TO CODE CIV. PROC., 473 (D). (ELLARD V. CONWAY (2001) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 540, 544, [" A DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST A DEFENDANT WHO WAS NOT SERVED WITH A SUMMONS IN THE MANNER PRESCRIBED BY STATUTE IS VOID."]). COUNSEL FOR MS. WONG IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE ORDER IS TO BE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ANNE-CRISTINE MASSULLO, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. =(302/ACM)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-08-2019 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Punitive Damage Allegations
Off Calendar Oct-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT SHI KUANG, ALLISON WONG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Punitive Damage Allegations is Off calendar per counsel's request. Notice filed on September 4, 2019.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-23-2019 Set For Trial On Dec-02-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-07-2018 Continued To Feb-06-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Shi Yuan Kuang and Allison Wong's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations and prayer in the complaint filed by plaintiff Roberto Barajas is denied. While perhaps not artfully alleged, liberally construed, the allegation that Mr. Barajas was told that Ms. Kuang consumed alcohol prior to the collision adequately alleges that Ms. Kuang acted despicably and in willful disregard of the safety of those she might encounter when she was knowingly driving a vehicle in an impaired condition. Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-29-2018 Continued To Dec-26-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT ALLISON WONG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST ALLISON WONG.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Allison Wong's motion to vacate default is GRANTED. In light of the ruling on defendant Allison Wong's motion to quash service of summons, the default is vacated pursuant to Code Civ. Proc., 473 (d). (Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544, [" a default judgment entered against a defendant who was not served with a summons in the manner prescribed by statute is void."]). Counsel for Ms. Wong is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. The order is to be in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Anne-Cristine Massullo, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/ACM)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT SHI KUANG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Shi Yuan Kuang's motion to quash summons is DENIED. Defendant Kuang fails to present any evidence supporting his motion. Kuang's motion set aside request for entry for default judgement, however, is GRANTED. Kuang's uncontroverted declaration support this court's finding that Kuang is unable to read or speak English and based on that, he was unaware that he was served with a summons. The declaration of Kuang's attorney supports this finding. There is sufficient evidence of excusable neglect presented. Code Civ. Proc., 473 (b). Setting aside the default also comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits especially where, as here, Plaintiff asserts no legal prejudice from granting the motion. Defendant Kwang must file his answer to the complaint no later than September 19, 2018. Counsel for Mr. Kuang is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. The order is to be in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Anne-Cristine Massullo, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/ACM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANTS SHI KUANG AND ALLISON WONG'S MOTION TO QUASH SUMMONS.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Alison Wong's motion to quash summons is GRANTED. Pursuant to Evid. Code 647, use of a California registered process server affects the burden of producing evidence of improper service and there is a rebuttable presumption that service was proper. See Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1441-1442. Plaintiff used a California registered process server and the rebuttable presumption applies. In her declaration, Wong states that she resided with a couple in their sixties and no person fitting the description on the proof of summons resided with her at the relevant time. Further, she affirms that she first became aware of the lawsuit when contacted by the attorney for Progressive Insurance. This evidence rebuts the presumption of proper service. No evidence has been presented by Plaintiff showing that the person served was a member of Wong's household. (See Fernandes v. Singh (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 932, 941, n.6, ["a declaration of non-service if credited by the trial court can rebut the presumption of proper service."]). The requirements of Code Civ. Proc., 415.20 (b) have not been satisfied as to Defendant Wong. The default as to Wong is hereby vacated pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. 473(d). (Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544, [" a default judgment entered against a defendant who was not served with a summons in the manner prescribed by statute is void."]). Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. The order is to be in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
